entities: AUGMENTED REALITY AND VIRTUAL REALITY INTERFACES
relations: Sumer contributed to the development of early writing systems
Egypt contributed to the development of early writing systems
China contributed to the development of early writing systems
Cuneiform was used by the ancient Sumerians in Mesopotamia
Hieroglyphics were used as a writing system in ancient Egypt
While Champollion's work was unrelated to China, both represent significant developments in the history of writing systems
Greece and China developed distinct writing systems that significantly impacted the evolution of written communication
Chinese characters are a significant part of China's writing system evolution
Cuneiform was developed and used in the region of Mesopotamia by the ancient Sumerians
Hieroglyphics were used as a writing system by the ancient Egyptian civilization
Champollion's decipherment of hieroglyphics provided new insights into the ancient Egyptian civilization
Greece developed the first true alphabet, influencing many modern writing systems
The Latin alphabet, used in English and most European languages, was derived from the Greek alphabet
The alphabet originated in the ancient Near East before being adapted by the Greeks
Digital technology's rise facilitated the development and adoption of Unicode for global text exchange
Emojis and emoticons are products of digital technology, representing new forms of digital communication
Augmented and virtual reality interfaces are advancements in digital technology, shaping the future of how we interact with written content
